gdpr_reasons: Visitor tracking: Google Tag Manager, MailChimp, MailChimp for WordPress:4.12.1

findings.description: Missing HSTS header (HTTP Strict Transport Security)
findings.risk: Browsers are not instructed to always use HTTPS. On unsecured networks (public WiFi), a visitor's first connection could be intercepted before the redirect to HTTPS occurs.

findings.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser has no restrictions on which scripts can run on the page. If the site is compromised, injected scripts can operate without constraint.

findings.description: Missing X-Frame-Options header
findings.risk: The website can be embedded in frames on other sites. This enables clickjacking attacks where users interact with hidden elements overlaid on the legitimate page.

findings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options header
findings.risk: Browsers may misinterpret uploaded files as executable content. This is primarily relevant if the site accepts file uploads.

findings.description: Missing Permissions-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser is not instructed to restrict access to device features (camera, microphone, geolocation). Third-party scripts embedded on the page could request these permissions.

findings.description: Missing Referrer-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser may send the full page URL to third-party sites when visitors click links. This can leak internal page paths or query parameters.

findings.description: X-Powered-By header discloses technology: PHP/8.5.4
findings.risk: The server advertises its backend technology in HTTP headers. This gives attackers specific version information to look up known vulnerabilities.

findings.description: WordPress version 4.12.1 publicly disclosed
findings.risk: The exact WordPress version is visible to anyone viewing the page source. This allows attackers to look up known vulnerabilities specific to this version and target them directly.

findings.description: Data-handling plugin detected: Contact Form 7
findings.risk: These plugins collect or process user data (form submissions, bookings, payments). If the site or plugin has a vulnerability, this data could be exposed. Keeping these plugins updated is critical for GDPR compliance.

findings.description: 8 WordPress plugins detected
findings.risk: Each plugin is additional code from a third-party developer. Outdated or abandoned plugins are a common entry point for attackers. Plugins should be reviewed and kept updated.

findings.description: Backend technology exposed: MySQL, PHP:8.5.4
findings.risk: The server advertises which backend technologies it runs. This gives attackers information about which exploits may be applicable. It does not mean the site is vulnerable, but it reduces the effort needed to find an attack vector.

findings.description: WordPress themes detected: Dt The7, Dt The7 Child
findings.risk: The active theme is visible in the HTML source. Outdated themes are a common source of vulnerabilities, similar to plugins.

findings.description: Outdated plugin: Svg Support (installed 2.5.8, latest 2.5.14)
findings.risk: The installed version of Svg Support is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.

findings.description: SVG Support [svg-support] < 2.5.11 (CVE-2024-10222)
findings.risk: CVE-2024-10222: WordPress SVG Support Plugin <= 2.5.10 is vulnerable to Cross Site Scripting (XSS)

findings.description: SVG Support [svg-support] < 2.5.9 (CVE-2022-23638)
findings.risk: CVE-2022-23638: SVG Support <= 2.5.8 - Stored Cross-Site Scripting via Vulnerability Dependency

findings.description: MC4WP: Mailchimp for WordPress [mailchimp-for-wp] >= 4.9.9 - <= 4.9.16 (CVE-2024-8850)
findings.risk: CVE-2024-8850: MC4WP: Mailchimp for WordPress 4.9.9 - 4.9.16 - Reflected Cross-Site Scripting

findings.description: Elementor Website Builder – More Than Just a Page Builder [elementor] >= 3.6.0 - <= 3.6.2 (CVE-2022-1329)
findings.risk: CVE-2022-1329: WordPress Elementor Website Builder plugin <= 3.6.2 - Arbitrary File Upload vulnerability

findings.risk: CVE-2023-47504: WordPress  Elementor Website Builder Plugin  <= 3.16.4 is vulnerable to Broken Access Control

findings.risk: CVE-2023-48777: WordPress  Elementor Website Builder Plugin  3.3.0-3.18.1 is vulnerable to Arbitrary File Upload

findings.risk: CVE-2024-24934: Elementor <= 3.19.0 - Authenticated(Contributor+) Arbitrary File Deletion and PHAR Deserialization

findings.risk: CVE-2023-23888: WordPress  Rank Math SEO Plugin  <= 1.0.107.2 is vulnerable to Local File Inclusion
